summary:
Marimastat has no established role in treating small-cell lung cancer based on available clinical data; it was investigated mainly outside this context such as in arthritis rather than oncology. Current management strategies focus instead on conventional therapies including platinum-based chemotherapies combined with etoposide.

Note that you are only supposed to summarize Agent1’s reasoning — do NOT add external knowledge beyond what he said.

Marimastat lacks support in clinical trials for small-cell lung cancer and its use in this setting isn't endorsed by existing literature. Standard care remains focused on chemotherapy regimens involving platinum agents and etoposide.
